skill.name: context-optimization
skill.description: Optimize AI context windows through compaction, masking, KV-cache, and partitioning strategies to maximize effective context capacity.
skill.summary: Maximize AI context capacity through compression and caching strategies
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 16 static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation/guide containing code examples for context optimization. Python code snippets were incorrectly flagged as shell commands, and text patterns like 'MD5' in '3+ turns' and skill names were misidentified as security issues. No actual security risks present.

content.value_statement: Context windows limit what AI models can process at once. This skill provides techniques to maximize effective context capacity through compaction, masking, KV-cache optimization, and partitioning, effectively doubling or tripling what your AI can handle.
actual_capabilities: Apply compaction strategies to summarize and compress context when approaching limits
Implement observation masking to replace verbose tool outputs with compact references
Optimize KV-cache usage to reuse cached computations across requests
Partition work across sub-agents with isolated contexts for better scalability
Design explicit context budgets and trigger-based optimization thresholds
Measure and monitor optimization effectiveness with token utilization metrics
limitations: Does not increase actual model context window limits
Quality degradation may occur with aggressive compression
Requires careful tuning of optimization thresholds for each use case
Some techniques require architectural support from the AI framework

use_cases.title: Long-running AI Agents
use_cases.description: Build production AI agents that maintain context over extended sessions without hitting token limits

use_cases.title: Large Document Processing
use_cases.description: Process documents larger than the context window by partitioning and aggregating results
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers and document processing teams

use_cases.title: Cost Reduction
use_cases.description: Reduce API costs by minimizing token usage through caching and compression strategies

prompt_templates.title: Basic Context Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check the current context utilization. If it exceeds 70%, apply compaction by summarizing older messages and preserving key decisions.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting to optimize a long conversation

prompt_templates.title: Tool Output Masking
prompt_templates.prompt: For tool outputs from 3+ turns ago that have served their purpose, replace them with compact references containing only key findings.
prompt_templates.scenario: Reducing token usage from verbose tool outputs

prompt_templates.title: Cache-Friendly Ordering
prompt_templates.prompt: Reorder context elements to maximize cache hits: place system prompt and tool definitions first, then reusable content, then unique content last.

prompt_templates.title: Sub-Agent Partitioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Split the current task into independent subtasks. Assign each to a separate sub-agent with isolated context. Aggregate results after all complete.
prompt_templates.scenario: Handling complex tasks that exceed context capacity

output_examples.input: Context utilization at 85%, conversation is becoming slow and expensive
output_examples.output: Applied compaction: summarized 20 previous messages into 3 key decisions, reduced context by 60%. Triggered observation masking on tool outputs from turns 1-5.

output_examples.input: Need to process a 50-page document with 32k context window
output_examples.output: Partitioned document into 4 sections. Assigned each to isolated sub-agent. Aggregated results: all sections processed, final summary fits in 8k tokens.

output_examples.input: System prompt and tool definitions repeat in every request
output_examples.output: Reordered context: system prompt first, then tool definitions, then conversation. Achieved 75% cache hit rate, reducing latency by 40%.

best_practices: Measure before optimizing - establish baseline token usage and performance metrics
Apply compaction before masking - summarization preserves more signal than removal
Design for cache stability - use consistent formatting and avoid dynamic content in prompts
anti_patterns: Aggressive compression - compressing below 50% causes significant quality loss
Masking critical observations - never mask data needed for current reasoning
Ignoring monitoring - optimization effectiveness degrades over time without measurement

faq.question: Does this skill actually increase the context window?
faq.answer: No. This skill optimizes how you use the available context, making it feel larger by removing redundancy and compressing data.

faq.question: What is the best optimization strategy for conversation-heavy tasks?
faq.answer: Compaction with summarization works best. Summarize old conversation turns while preserving key decisions and commitments.

faq.question: How much token reduction can I expect?
faq.answer: Compaction achieves 50-70% reduction with less than 5% quality loss. Masking achieves 60-80% reduction in masked observations.

faq.question: Does caching work across different conversations?
faq.answer: Prefix caching only works when prompts have identical prefixes. Keep system prompts stable to maximize cache hits.

faq.question: When should I use sub-agent partitioning?
faq.answer: Partition when a task is too complex for one context, or when subtasks have conflicting context requirements.

faq.question: How do I know when to trigger optimization?
faq.answer: Monitor token utilization above 80%, response quality degradation, or increasing latency as primary triggers.
